statement: contract-policy-check v1 is active (validator for .episteme/policy.json mirroring ledger-check)
no policy validator exists yet; tools/ledger-check.py is the structural pattern to mirror (argparse + jsonschema Draft202012Validator + per-line/structural checks)
a policy may claim status:ready only when every candidate_steps[].rests_on id maps to a verified ledger entry
a policy may claim status:ready only when every candidate_steps[].rests_on AND recommended_next_action.rests_on id maps to a verified ledger entry
schemas/policy.schema.json + tools/policy-check.py implemented; all 5 contract-policy-check ACs are green and the live .episteme/policy.json validates against the new schema
oracle_ref: 5/5 AC oracles meet expected exit codes

statement: verdict.json has no validator and the PRD assumption->ledger through-line has no checker; ledger-check.py + policy-check.py are the pattern to mirror for both
Episteme validators must be pure-local: no network and no LLM call (deterministic cheap oracles only)
the PRD assumption convention is an inline [ASSUMPTION: ...] tag plus an Assumptions Index section; prd-check parses that
verdict-check.py mirrors the ledger-check/policy-check pattern: argparse + jsonschema Draft202012Validator + exit 0/1/2; schemas/verdict.schema.json is the source of truth
prd-check scans [ASSUMPTION ...] tags via regex and cross-checks for a matching assumption/assumed ledger entry; exact tag grammar pending a spike
keep verdict-check and prd-check self-contained; do not extract a shared validator helper under this feature (silence over speculation - no evidence duplication hurts)
verdict-check.py + verdict.schema.json implemented; story-01 ACs all green and the live .episteme/verdict.json validates
oracle_ref: 3/3 verdict-check oracles meet expected exit codes

statement: PRD assumption grammar CONFIRMED: inline [ASSUMPTION: <text>] (colon+text) tags + an '## Assumptions Index' table whose rows map each assumption to a led-NNNN; bare [ASSUMPTION] (no colon) in prose are convention-references, not real assumptions. prd-check keys off the Assumptions Index led-NNNN refs.
evidence_for: templates/prd.md uses [ASSUMPTION: ...] colon form + ## Assumptions Index

statement: prd-check.py implemented; story-03 ACs green and the live .episteme/prd.md passes (its led-0008 Assumptions-Index ref is a ledgered assumption)
prd-check keys off the Assumptions Index led-NNNN refs; each must exist as a type:assumption ledger entry (parse plan now confirmed by green oracle, was led-0010 assumed)
contract-prd-check v2 active: added AC-3 (no orphan inline [ASSUMPTION:] tag - inline count must be <= Assumptions Index led-ref count); v2 supersedes v1 which only enforced check B

prd-check hardened to enforce check A: scans real inline [ASSUMPTION: <text>] tags (ignoring backtick code spans and literal '...' meta-references), requires count(inline) <= count(led-NNNN refs in ## Assumptions Index); the v1 hole that let an inline tag pass without an Index row is closed
oracle_ref: AC-3 GREEN: prd-check rejects prd_inline_orphan.md (2 inline, 1 indexed) naming the orphan; AC-1/AC-2 regression-free; live .episteme/prd.md still passes

statement: verdict.schema.json hardened: required fields now include kind, reason, progress_delta, flags, invalidates_hypothesis (was only kind+reason); flags enum closed to ORACLE_PASSED/FAILED/PREDICTION_MISMATCH/REGRESSION/STATE_REVISITED. Catches partial verdicts that previously slipped through.
oracle_ref: partial verdict {kind,reason} only -> exit 1 naming 3 missing fields; 3 contract-verdict-check ACs regression-free; live .episteme/verdict.json OK
